title: NPTEL IoT 2023 JAN

questions.question: Which of the following is/are the characteristics of IoT?

options.text: Abundance of sleeping nodes, mobile and non-IP device

questions.detailed_solution:
Characteristics of IoT are -
a. Efficient, scalable and associated architecture.
b. Unambiguous naming and addressing.
c. Abundance of sleeping nodes, mobile and non-IP device.
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 16:06

questions.question: A ___________ allows us to use our smartphones to lock and unlock our door remotely at our homes or our businesses.
questions.detailed_solution:
Smartphones can be used to lock and unlock doors remotely, and
business owners can change key codes rapidly to grant or restrict access to employees and
guests.
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 25:35

questions.question: The function/functions of an IoT Gateway is/are to?

options.text: Forward packets between LAN and WAN and on the IP layer
Connect IoT LAN to a WAN

questions.detailed_solution:
An IoT Gateway is a router connecting the IoT LAN to a WAN to the
Internet, can implement several LAN and WAN, Forwards packets between LAN and WAN
and on the IP layer.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 04:44

questions.question: Multi-homing is the concept where a node can be connected to multiple networks for _________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Multi-homing is a concept where a node or an IoT device or a sub-
network can be connected to multiple networks for improving the reliability.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 15:27

questions.question: A Passive Infrared Ray (PIR) sensor is used for ____________?
questions.detailed_solution:
Passive Infrared Ray (PIR) sensor can be used to detect if there is any
obstacle.

questions.question: For which of the following, Vector Sensors are required to measure or sense them?
questions.detailed_solution:
Vector Sensors produce output signal or voltage which is generally
proportional to the magnitude, direction, as well as the orientation of the quantity being
measured. Physical quantities such as sound, image, velocity, acceleration, orientation, etc.
are all vector quantities, as only their magnitude is not sufficient to convey the complete
information.

questions.question: The sensitivity of a sensor under real conditions may differ from the value specified. This is called _____________?
questions.detailed_solution:
The sensitivity of a sensor under real conditions may differ from the
value specified. This is called sensitivity error.

questions.question: A random deviation of the signal that varies in time is called ____________.
questions.detailed_solution: Noise is a random deviation of the signal that varies in time.

questions.question: A Relay Switch is an example of _____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Relay Switch is an example of an actuator. It is an electromechanical
switch that can be used to perform On/Off operations of electrical appliances.

questions.question: What is a Pneumatic Actuator?

options.text: It is a type of actuator driven by compressed air or vacuum
It is a type of actuator driven by fluid
It is a type of actuator driven by solid

text: It is a type of actuator driven by compressed air or vacuum

questions.detailed_solution:
A pneumatic actuator converts energy formed by vacuum or
compressed air at high pressure into either linear or rotary motion.
questions.answer_display: It is a type of actuator driven by compressed air or vacuum

questions.question: Which type of actuators tend to be compact, lightweight, economical, and with high power density?
questions.detailed_solution:
Thermal or Magnetic Actuators can be actuated by applying thermal or
magnetic energy. They tend to be compact, lightweight, economical, and with high power
density.

questions.question: Polymer based actuators designed to handle fragile objects like fruit harvesting in agriculture or manipulating internal organs in biomedicine are called?
questions.detailed_solution:
Soft Actuators are polymer-based actuators designed to handle fragile
objects like fruit harvesting in agriculture or manipulating internal organs in biomedicine.

questions.question: Full form of SMP is _______________________?
questions.detailed_solution:
Shape Memory Polymer (SMP) actuators function similar to our
muscles, even providing a response to a range of stimuli such as light, electrical, magnetic,
heat, pH, and moisture changes.

questions.question: Duty Cycling of the sensors is managed by which component of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Power Management Unit does things like duty cycling of sensors that is
how much time they are to be powered on and how much time they will be off.
questions.reference: See lecture 5 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part - I) @ 13:39

questions.question: Which of the following are challenges of IoT
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT Challenges are -
 Security
 Modeling and Analysis
 Complexity Management
 Scalability
 Energy Efficiency
 Interfacing
 Interoperability
 Data Storage
 Data Analytics
questions.reference: See lecture 5 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part - I) @ 24:40

questions.question: Which of the following is based on the publish-subscribe model?
questions.detailed_solution:
Message Queue Telemetry Transport (MQTT) is a publish-subscribe
based lightweight messaging protocol for use in conjunction with TCP/IP protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 01:51

questions.question: In MQTT, a topic to which a client is subscribed is updated in the form of messages and distributed by the ____________________?
questions.detailed_solution:
A topic to which a client is subscribed is updated in the form of
messages and distributed by the message broker.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 03:33

questions.question: Which of the following is used when more than one level needs to be subscribed, such as the entire sub-tree, i.e., a multilevel wildcard?
questions.detailed_solution:
If more than one level needs to be subscribed, such as the entire sub-
tree, there is also a multilevel wildcard (#). It allows to subscribe to all underlying hierarchy
levels. For example house/# is subscribing to all topics beginning with house.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 12:40

questions.question: The CoAP protocol is designed for -.
questions.detailed_solution:
CoAP - Constrained Application Protocol. It is a web transfer protocol
for use with constrained nodes and networks. Designed for Machine-to-Machine (M2M)
applications such as smart energy and building automation. Based on Request-Response
model between end-points.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 00:35

questions.question: Statement I - The messaging sub-layer of CoAP, is responsible for reliability and duplication of messages. Statement II - The request/response sub-layer is responsible for communication. Which of the above statement/statements is/are correct?

options.text: Statement I Correct but Statement II Incorrect

questions.detailed_solution:
The messaging sub-layer of CoAP, is responsible for reliability and
duplication of messages while the request/response sub-layer is responsible for
communication.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 04:19

questions.question: Which of the following is a messaging mode in CoAP?
questions.detailed_solution:
CoAP has four messaging modes
 Confirmable
 Non-Confirmable
 Piggyback
 Separate
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 05:29

questions.question: Which of the following provides for the discovery of services residing locally or across a network?
questions.detailed_solution:
XMPP provides for the discovery of services residing locally or across a
network, and the availability information of these services.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 12:03

questions.question: AMQP is a protocol of which layer?
questions.detailed_solution: AMQP is an Application Layer protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 01:41

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the AMQP protocol?
questions.detailed_solution:
Features of AMQP are -
 Security
 Reliability
 Interoperability
 Routing
 Queuing
 Open Standard
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 04:54

questions.question: There are a total of _________ number of AMQP frame types are defined that are used to initiate, control, and tear down the transfer of messages between two peers.
questions.detailed_solution:
Nine AMQP frame types are defined that are used to initiate, control,
and tear down the transfer of messages between two peers.
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 06:34

questions.question: The function/functions of the Queue component of the AMQP protocol is/are -

options.text: Receive messages and route them to queues
Separate queues for separate business process

questions.detailed_solution:
The functions of the Queue components are to -
 Separate queues for separate business process
 Consumer receive messages from queues
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 08:05

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are false? Statement - I: IEEE 802.15.4 is a well-known standard for low data-rate Wireless Personal Area Network (WPAN). Statement - II: IEEE 802.15.4 standard operates in the ISM band.
questions.detailed_solution:
IEEE 802.15.4 is a well known standard for low data-rate Wireless
Personal Area Network (WPAN). It operates in the ISM band.

questions.question: The IEEE 802.15.4 establishes functionalities in which layers?
questions.detailed_solution:
The IEEE 802.15.4 is useful for establishing functionalities in the
Physical and Data Link Layers.

questions.question: Which is not a purpose of the ZigBee Device Object (ZDO)?
questions.detailed_solution:
The functions of the ZigBee Device Object (ZDO) are -
 Device Management
 Security
 Policies

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: Similar to traditional barcodes and QR codes, RFID tag data cannot be read outside the line-of-sight.
questions.detailed_solution:
As compared to traditional barcodes and QR codes, RFID tag data can
be read outside the line-of-sight.

questions.question: Choose the correct option based on the following two statements on the HART Physical Layer. Statement-I: It is derived from the IEEE 802.15.4 protocol. Statement-II: It operated only in the 2.4 GHz ISM band.

options.text: Both Statements I and II are False
Both Statements I and II are True

text: Both Statements I and II are True

questions.detailed_solution:
HART Physical Layer is derived from the IEEE 802.15.4 protocol. It
operated in the 2.4 GHz ISM band.
questions.answer_display: Both Statements I and II are True

questions.question: Which of the following characteristic of HART Data Link Layer helps to increase reliability and security?
questions.detailed_solution:
HART Data Link Layers incorporates channel hopping and channel
blacklisting to increase reliability and security.

questions.question: State True or False Statement: Channel blacklisting in HART identifies channels consistently affected by interference and removes them from use.
questions.detailed_solution:
Channel blacklisting in HART identifies channels consistently affected
by interference and removes them from use.

questions.question: At the MAC layer - Statement-I: WirelessHART utilizes Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA). Statement-II: ZigBee applies Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection (CSMA/CD).

options.text: Both Statements I and II are False
Both Statements I and II are True

text: Both Statements I and II are True

questions.detailed_solution:
At the MAC layer, WirelessHART utilizes Time Division Multiple
Access (TDMA), allotting individual time slots for each transmission. ZigBee applies
Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection (CSMA/CD).
questions.answer_display: Both Statements I and II are True

questions.question: NFC works on the principal of
questions.detailed_solution: NFC works on the principle of magnetic induction.

questions.question: Bluetooth technology is based on Ad-hoc technology also known as?
questions.detailed_solution:
Bluetooth technology is based on Ad-hoc technology also known as Ad-
hoc Piconets.

questions.question: Class 2 Bluetooth radios have a range of about?
questions.detailed_solution:
Class 2 radios are most commonly found in mobile devices and have a
range of 10 meters.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a phase in Bluetooth connection establishment?
questions.detailed_solution:
The three phases of Bluetooth connection establishment are -
 Inquiry
 Paging
 Connection

questions.question: Zwave can support ___________ number of nodes in a network?
questions.detailed_solution:
In Zwave, mesh network topology is the main mode of operation, and
can support 232 nodes in a network.

questions.question: Topologies allowed in ISA 100.11A are?
questions.detailed_solution: The ISA 100.11A support the Mesh and Star/Tree topologies.

questions.question: An example of an Operating System (OS) that a sensor node can have is?
questions.detailed_solution: Sensor nodes can have OS such as TinyOS.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a constraint on sensor nodes?
questions.detailed_solution:
Constraints on sensor nodes -
 Must consume extremely low power
 Be autonomous
 Be adaptive to the environment

questions.question: Nodes in WSNs that exhibit features of failed nodes but they can also send false routing messages which are a threat to the integrity of the network are called?
questions.detailed_solution:
Nodes in WSNs that exhibit features of failed nodes but they can also
send false routing messages which are a threat to the integrity of the network are called
Badly Failed Nodes.

questions.question: Which of the following are the two popular schemes to re-establish the connectivity between dumb nodes with others?
questions.detailed_solution:
CoRD and CoRAD are the two popular schemes to re-establish the
connectivity between dumb nodes with others.

questions.question: Full form of WBAN is?
questions.detailed_solution: Wireless Body Area Network (WBAN).

questions.question: When an intruder enters into the agricultural field through the boundary (perimeter) of the field, a/an ___________ sensor can be used to detect the object and a/an _____________ sensor can be used to sense the distance at which the object is located.
questions.detailed_solution:
When an intruder enters into the agricultural field through the boundary
(perimeter) of the field, a PIR sensor can be used to detect the object and an Ultrasonic
sensor can be used to sense the distance at which the object is located.

questions.question: ____________ is defined as, all the nodes are connected in the network, so that sensed data can reach to sink node?
questions.detailed_solution:
Connectivity is defined as, all the nodes are connected in the network,
so that sensed data can reach to sink node.

questions.question: The objective of coverage in WSN is.

options.text: To use a minimum number of sensors and maximize the network lifetime
To use a maximum number of sensors and maximize the network lifetime
To use a minimum number of sensors and minimize the network lifetime
To use a maximum number of sensors and minimize the network lifetime

text: To use a minimum number of sensors and maximize the network lifetime

questions.detailed_solution:
The objective of coverage is to use a minimum number of sensors and
maximize the network lifetime.
questions.answer_display: To use a minimum number of sensors and maximize the network lifetime

questions.question: State True or False Statement-I: If transmission range ≤ 2* sensing range, coverage implies connectivity.
questions.detailed_solution: If transmission range ≥ 2* sensing range, coverage implies connectivity.

questions.question: A mobile entity that collects the data from sensor nodes goes to the sink, and delivers the collected data from different sensor nodes are called?
questions.detailed_solution:
A mobile entity that collects the data from sensor nodes goes to the sink,
and delivers the collected data from different sensor nodes are called Data Mules.

questions.question: Human-centric Sensing is possible because of?
questions.detailed_solution:
Human-centric Sensing is possible because of Smartphone, PDAs,
Miniaturization, and Proliferation of devices.

questions.question: Which of the following are the roles played by humans in Human-centric Sensing?
questions.detailed_solution:
The three distinct roles (not necessarily mutually exclusive) played by
humans are -
 Sensing Targets
 Sensor Operators
 Data Source

questions.question: Which of the following is/are NOT goal of Participatory Sensing?

options.text: Not just collect data, but allow common people to access data and share knowledge

questions.detailed_solution:
The goal of Participatory Sensing is not just collect data, but allow
common people to access data and share knowledge.

questions.question: The two popular network topologies in UAV networks are
questions.detailed_solution:
The two popular network topologies in UAV networks are Mesh and
Star topologies.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an issue in UAV networks?

options.text: Relative position of UAV may change

questions.detailed_solution:
Key issues in UAV networks are -
 Frequently change in network topology
 Relative position of UAV may change
 Malfunctioning of UAV
 Intermittent link nature

questions.question: The scalability in single UAV system as compared to multi-UAV system is?
questions.detailed_solution:
The scalability in single UAV system as compared to multi-UAV
system is limited.

questions.question: Typical types of mesh networks in UAV networks are
questions.detailed_solution:
Typical types of mesh networks in UAV networks are -
 Flat Mesh
 Hierarchical Mesh

questions.question: State True or False Statement-I: SCADA is designed for isolated systems using proprietary solutions, whereas M2M is designed for cross-platform integration.
questions.detailed_solution:
SCADA is designed for isolated systems using proprietary solutions,
whereas M2M is designed for cross-platform integration.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of M2M?

options.text: Large number of nodes or devices

questions.detailed_solution:
Features of M2M -
 Large number of nodes or devices
 Low cost
 Energy efficient
 Small traffic per machine/device

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an M2M node type?
questions.detailed_solution:
The three node types of M2M are -
 Low end node
 Mid end node
 High end node

questions.question: Suppose that an IoT sensor node A works on wireless WiFi and senses pressure as a physical parameter in integer number, and another IoT node B operates on IEEE 802.15.4 Zigbee and senses humidity as a floating point (decimal) number. In this context, which among the following correctly describes the issues with the deployment.
questions.detailed_solution:
Since the two sensors sense different parameters in different formats and communicates
with different protocols, this is an issue of both heterogeneity and interoperability.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 21 on introduction to interoperability

questions.question: RESTful web services are utilized for _________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Service-oriented computing-based architecture, RESTful web services, open
standard protocols (IEEE 802.15.4), and closed protocols (Z-wave) are the popular approaches
utilized towards syntactic interoperability for device interaction.

questions.question: Which UMB interoperability component is responsible for converting physical devices into virtually abstracted ones?
questions.detailed_solution: UMB-A is responsible for converting physical devices into virtually abstracted ones.

questions.question: User interoperability is the interoperability problem between an user and ____________.
questions.detailed_solution: In user interoperability, the issue of interoperability is between the user and device.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 21, ppt No. 7, types of interoperability

questions.question: Which of the following is an open, global, multi-sector standard for efficient, accurate, flexible classification of products and services?
questions.detailed_solution:
UNSPC stands for United Nations Standard Products and Services Code. It is
one of the device classification solutions. eCl@ss is the standard utilized for classification and clear
description of cross-industry products. Electronic Product Code (EPC) is used to generate unique
addresses for device identification.

questions.question: What does the routing component in UMB-C use for routing the metadata messages?
questions.detailed_solution:
UMB-C uses Middleware Routing Table for routing metadata messages among the
devices.

questions.question: Which among the following are components of an Arduino UNO Board?
questions.detailed_solution:
An Arduino UNO board contains several components, which also contain the
ones listed above.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 22 on Arduino Board details.

questions.question: The tool used to select a particular COM port for connecting Arduino to a serial connector is called a sketch.
questions.detailed_solution: Sketch in Arduino is the program that is coded in Arduino IDE.

questions.question: Which kind of conflict occur when different processing logic are applied to same IoT networked devices or applications?
questions.detailed_solution:
Semantic conflict occurs when different processing logics are applied to same
IoT networked devices or applications.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE for the sketch command given below?

options.text: Provides a delay of 3000 nano seconds
Provides a delay of 3000 simulation time

questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Arduino programming.

questions.question: How many types of loops will you find in Arduino Programming?
questions.detailed_solution:
Like C programming, Arduino sketches also have 3 types of loops, for, while and do-
while loops.

questions.question: How many digital I/O pins are there in Arduino Uno.
questions.detailed_solution:
From the basics of Arduino Uno.Refer to the book: S. Misra,
A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT. Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Servo motors that you connect to Arduino, are an example of
questions.detailed_solution:
Servo motors, along with other different types of motors are examples of Actuators in
IoT network.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 25 on Integration of Arduino with Sensors and Actuators.

questions.question: Which of the following functions exist by default in Arduino IDE?
questions.detailed_solution: By default Arduino IDE consists of 2 functions - setup() and loop().

questions.question: Choose the right option for if/conditional operator.
questions.detailed_solution:
Conditional operator may also be written as Val = (condition)?(Statement 1):(Statement
2).

questions.question: Python's installation comes with an integrated development environment for programming.
questions.detailed_solution: As per Python program.

questions.detailed_solution: As per Python program.

questions.detailed_solution: As per Python program

questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming.

questions.question: The variable that is declared inside the function in Python is called a Global variable.
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 25:58)

questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming.

questions.question: Which of the following libraries in Python is used for processing images.
questions.detailed_solution:
In python programming PIL, OpenCV, Pillow are common image
processing libraries.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @ 18:08 )

questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming

questions.question: Which of the following shortcut exits the nano editor?
questions.detailed_solution: Ctrl + O writes the code to a file. Ctrl + X exits the nano editor.

questions.question: Which of the following is an unordered data type in Python?
questions.detailed_solution: As per basics of Python programming.

questions.question: Sensors can be analog or digital. Is the statement true?
questions.detailed_solution: Sensors can be analog or digital.

questions.question: Is relay a type of mechanical switch?
questions.detailed_solution: Relay is a type of mechanical/electromechanical switch.

questions.question: Which of the following converts energy to motion?
questions.detailed_solution: Actuator converts energy to motion.

questions.question: Python does not follow strict indentation.
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 7:47)

questions.question: Functions cannot be reassigned to the variables in Python.
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 24:54)

questions.question: The switches in a non-Software Defined Network (SDN) environment do not have a global view of the network.
questions.detailed_solution: Switches in non-SDN do not have global view of the network.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a function of the Application Plane in Software Defined Network architecture?
questions.detailed_solution: The Data Plane in SDN is responsible for data forwarding.

questions.question: In Socket programming, the parameter AF_INET stands for ___________.
During remote server access by a Raspberry Pi, where the Raspberry Pi acts as a client, the client needs the following?

options.text: Both server IP address and port number

text: Both server IP address and port number

questions.detailed_solution:
A client can communicate with a server only if both IP address and port numbers are
known.
questions.answer_display: Both server IP address and port number

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Controllers in SDN receive requests from the _______________.
questions.detailed_solution:
SDN controllers should be able to handle all requests from the SDN switches (Please
refer

questions.question: During remote server access using socket programming what is the utility of the <socket_name>.listen() function?

options.text: To bind the socket to connection
To wait for clients to connect

text: To wait for clients to connect

questions.detailed_solution: listen() function makes the server wait for incoming client connections
questions.answer_display: To wait for clients to connect

questions.question: With respect to client-server model of socket programming, in which of the following does the function <socket_name>.bind() reside?
questions.detailed_solution:
The bind() function binds the socket name to the socket connection at the server
side

questions.question: Which among the following are valid data processing activities
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @19:44).

questions.question: Which among the following is the correct direction for PACKET_IN type messages in SDN?
questions.detailed_solution:
PACKET_IN messages are sent from switches to the controller upon receipt of
new unknown packets.

questions.question: Suppose a particular flow-rule has a soft time-out of 5s and a hard time-out of 3s. Is this association correct?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hard time-outs of flow rules are always greater than soft time-outs, not the other
way round.

questions.question: In SDN Backup Controllers are required for which among the following?

options.text: To act as backup of the main controller all the time
To act as backup when the main controller fails
To replace the main controller all together

text: To act as backup when the main controller fails

questions.detailed_solution: Backup controllers acts as backup when the main controller is down
questions.answer_display: To act as backup when the main controller fails

questions.question: Which of the following is true?

options.text: Traditional Network: Routing Table, Software Defined Network: Routing Table
Traditional Network: Flow Table, Software Defined Network: Routing Table
Traditional Network: Routing Table, Software Defined Network: Flow Table
Traditional Network: Flow Table, Software Defined Network: Flow Table

text: Traditional Network: Routing Table, Software Defined Network: Flow Table

questions.detailed_solution:
All switches in traditional network have routing tables and those in Software Defined
Network have flow tables
questions.answer_display: Traditional Network: Routing Table, Software Defined Network: Flow Table

questions.question: What are the benefits of using SDN over IoT?

options.text: Management of end-device mobility and dynamic flow rules
Software control of end-devices, i.e sensors and actuators

questions.detailed_solution:
SDN over IoT can be used to solve a plethora of issues and problems in a dynamic and
efficient manner.

questions.question: Sensor OpenFlow, Soft-WSN and SDN-WISE are examples of ____________.

options.text: Various SDN protocols for wired LANs
Various implementations of the concept of Software Defined IoT

text: Various implementations of the concept of Software Defined IoT

questions.detailed_solution:
The given names are all examples of various implementations of different concepts of
Software Defined IoT by various research groups across the world.
questions.answer_display: Various implementations of the concept of Software Defined IoT

questions.question: Consider the following python script using the split() function, what will be the correct print output (SEE the options VERY carefully including the quotation marks) dat = 'Apple,Guava#Banana' var = dat.split('#')
questions.detailed_solution: The split('#') method splits the string into two parts with respect to the '#' character.

questions.question: Which among the following are core challenges of traditional mobile networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
Traditional mobile networks suffer from a lot of problems, few of which are as given in
the options.

questions.question: Network virtualization enables ______________ of physical resources
questions.detailed_solution:
Abstraction of physical resources from network services is one of the core functionality
of virtualization.

questions.question: In comparison to Software-Defined Networks, traditional networks are cost expensive with respect to which of the following?
questions.detailed_solution:
Traditional networks are cost expensive with respect to both capex and opex in
comparison to SDN.

questions.question: General OpenFlow supports both wireless and wired connections.
questions.detailed_solution: A modified OpenFlow is necessary for supporting wireless connections.

questions.question: With respect to Software Defined Data Centre Networking (SD-DCN), ____________ can use wild cards and _________________ uses exact match rules.
questions.detailed_solution: Refer to ppt No. 19 of

questions.question: In cloud computing model, which among the following are characteristics of computing resources?
questions.detailed_solution:
"Cloud computing is a model for enabling convenient, on-demand network
access to a shared pool of configurable computing resources (e.g., network infrastructures, servers,
storage, applications, etc.)" - NIST.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 37, Introduction to Cloud Computing.

questions.question: Iaas, PaaS and SaaS are examples of what type of cloud models?
questions.detailed_solution: Iaas, PaaS and SaaS are three major types of cloud service models.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 37, Introduction to Cloud Computing

questions.question: An organization A wants to deploy a cloud infrastructure, whereby it wants to push majority of the data to a cloud whose servers can be situated anywhere within the globe, but it wants certain private data to be pushed only to cloud servers that are present on-premise and are accessible by only authenticated members of the organization. In this context which among the following deployment model should be used?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hybrid cloud deployment model supports both the features of public and private
cloud.

questions.question: Which of the following type of client requires constant communication/connection with the cloud server?
questions.detailed_solution:
A thin client is a network computer without a hard disk drive and high configurations.
They act as simple terminals and require constant communication with the servers.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Typically, cloud computing have _____ components?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud computing has 6 components. They are clients, services, applications, platforms,
storage, and infrastructure.

questions.question: What does 'CIA' in cloud data security stand for?
questions.detailed_solution: 'CIA' stands for 'Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability'.

questions.question: In SaaS model of cloud computing, which of the following acts as the thin-client?
questions.detailed_solution:
Web browsers at the client side act as thin-clients which makes connection to the cloud
to receive the services provided by the SaaS model.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 38 on SaaS, ppt No. 21.

questions.question: Data in __________ and Data at _______________ are two important aspects of data security
questions.detailed_solution:
Data in transit and Data at rest are two important aspects of data security within cloud
computing framework.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 39 on Data Security, ppt No. 18

questions.question: Which among the following is a metric for Service Level Agreement (SLA) in Cloud Computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
Availability, Portability, Response Time, Problem Reporting and Penalty are various
SLA metrics for Cloud Computing. Refer Chapter 10 (Page 245) of "Introduction to IoT" by Sudip Misra,
Anandarup Mukherjee, Arijit Roy, Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Which of the following cloud computing models does AWS EC2 belong to?
questions.detailed_solution: AWS EC2 is a popular example of IaaS.

questions.question: Openstack is a free open source software for cloud framework simulation and experimentation with various cloud applications.
questions.detailed_solution:
Openstack is a free open source software for cloud framework simulation and
experimentation with various cloud applications. It can be downloaded and installed for free.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a component of OpenStack.
questions.detailed_solution:
Plasma is not a component of OpenStack. The rest are various components, including
Nova, Glance, Swift etc.

questions.question: You cannot ping your OpenStack instance from an outside network unless you connect your instance with the public network through a ____________
questions.detailed_solution:
You are supposed to connect your virtual OpenStack instance with the public network
through a router

questions.question: Virtualized resources within the OpenStack simulator that you can define, set parameters of and deploy within OpenStack are also known as
questions.detailed_solution:
In OpenStack instances refer to the virtual resources that you define, initialize and
deploy.

questions.question: The SCSP in sensor clouds is responsible for caching the data in the databases.
questions.detailed_solution:
The SCSP in sensor clouds is responsible for caching the data in the databases. (Please
refer

questions.question: Select the correct option for replacing the box (in red) in the following architecture for sensor cloud user organization view.
questions.detailed_solution:
The browser interface sends template view and sensed information to the user
organization module in the sensor cloud user organization view.

questions.question: Which among the following is the principal feature of sensor clouds, with respect to sensor nodes?
questions.detailed_solution: Sensor virtualization is the principal feature of sensor clouds and their utility.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 42 and 43 on Sensor cloud

questions.question: Caching in sensor cloud provide no benefit over regions with slow environmental monitoring rate.
questions.detailed_solution:
To prevent unnecessarily energy wastage in continuous sensing operation, sensor
cloud uses caching.

questions.question: How many different types of caching mechanism are there in sensor cloud?
questions.detailed_solution:
Internal Cache (IC) and External Cache (EC) are two different types of caching
mechanisms used in sensor cloud.

questions.question: Virtual sensors within a sensor cloud have communication interfaces with _______________.

options.text: Only the physical sensors below them
Only the end-user applications above them
Both physical sensors below and applications above
Neither the physical sensors, nor the applications above

text: Both physical sensors below and applications above

questions.detailed_solution:
Virtual sensor layer sits in the middle having interface to both physical sensors as well
as applications.
questions.answer_display: Both physical sensors below and applications above

questions.question: Data from an IoT device is transferred to cloud via a network, which is then processed at the cloud and then a response is sent back to the IoT device from the cloud after processing. The time it takes for one-way data transfer between the node and cloud is 10s and the data processing time at the cloud is 'x' seconds. It takes a total of 25s for the entire to and fro transfer of data between the sensor and cloud along with processing at the cloud. What is the value of x?
questions.detailed_solution:
Time taken for one-way data transfer between the node and cloud is 10s. Total time
taken for the data transfer is 25s. So 25=10+x+10 (transfer from node to cloud+processing at
cloud+transfer from cloud to node). Thus x = 5s.

questions.question: In IoT, temporal sensitivity of data plays an important role
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT data can be classified in to time sensitive data, less time sensitive data and data not
sensitive to time. Hence time sensitivity plays a big role in IOT data classification.

questions.question: Extremely time sensitive data in IoT should be processed and analyzed ____________.

options.text: Distance between source and place of analysis does not matter

questions.detailed_solution:
Extremely time sensitive data should be processed and analyzed closest to the source,
so that prompt action can be taken immediately.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 44 on Fog Computing, ppt No. 17.

questions.question: Which among the following is a view of a Fog Computing Architecture?
questions.detailed_solution:
Fog Computing Architectural framework has several views, node, system and software
among them. Refer Chapter 11 (Page 260 - 263) of "Introduction to IoT" by Sudip Misra, Anandarup
Mukherjee, Arijit Roy, Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Which among the following is true?

options.text: Fog computing acts as a complement to cloud computing
Fog computing is a replacement for cloud computing
Fog computing and cloud computing are the same
Fog computing is more powerful than cloud computing (with respect to resources)

text: Fog computing acts as a complement to cloud computing

questions.detailed_solution:
Fog computing and cloud computing are complementary technologies. Fog helps in
bringing the cloud closer to the IoT devices.
questions.answer_display: Fog computing acts as a complement to cloud computing

questions.question: A smart city _____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
A smart city is an urban system, uses ICT and makes infrastructure more
reliable.

questions.question: Collective data is more intelligent than the single sources.
questions.detailed_solution:
Collective data is rich in information and generates better intelligence
than the single sources.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the challenges of Data fusion?
questions.detailed_solution:
The challenges of data fusion involves Imperfection, Conflicts and
Ambiguity.

questions.question: Which of the following are challenges for IoT in smart cities?
questions.detailed_solution:
Security and Reliability are two challenges of IoT in smart
cities.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ___________ combines information from multiple sensors.
questions.detailed_solution:
Data fusion combines information from multiple sensors. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following are the functional layers in smart parking?
questions.detailed_solution:
Information collection, System Deployment and Service
Dissemination are the three functional layers in smart parking

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about the different components present in a smart home? Statement I: Sensor networks and communication network infrastructure Statement II: Intelligent control and management Statement III: Manual instead of automatic access of devices
questions.detailed_solution:
The different components of smart home include communication
network infrastructure, Intelligent control and management, Sensor networks, smart
features and automatic response.

questions.question: Which of the following are included in the Service Dissemination in smart parking? Statement I: Dynamic Pricing Statement II: Infrastructure-free and infrastructure-based information Statement III: Parking Choice
questions.detailed_solution:
Service Dissemination in smart parking includes Dynamic Pricing,
parking choice and infrastructure- free information and infrastructure-based
information.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ is a network contained within a home.
questions.detailed_solution:
Home area network is a network contained within a home (Please
refer

questions.question: Fill in the blank. In Vehicle-to-Human / Human-to-Vehicle interaction, the human communicating with the vehicle ____________.

options.text: Is present in the same vehicle
Is present outside the vehicle on the roadside

text: Is present outside the vehicle on the roadside

questions.detailed_solution:
In V2H/H2V, the human is present outside the vehicle on the
roadside.
questions.answer_display: Is present outside the vehicle on the roadside

questions.question: Wired HAN provides easy integration with pre-existing house infrastructure.
questions.detailed_solution:
Wired HAN provides easy integration with pre-existing house
infrastructure like existing telephone systems, cables and so on

questions.question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true about Konnex?

options.text: Utilizes only short ranges in home
Does not have standards for building networks

questions.detailed_solution:
The Konnex is an important standard for home and building
networks. It utilizes full range of home communication. It must be setup and
configured before its proper usage

questions.question: Which of the following is the reason for failures of TCP/IP in V2X?

options.text: TCP/IP handles information exchange between multiple pair of entities
The increase in the number of wireless devices restricts the mobility
TCP/IP can identify the addresses of the endpoints
Information exchange does not depend on the location of the data

text: The increase in the number of wireless devices restricts the mobility

questions.detailed_solution:
The increase in the number of wireless devices restricts the
mobility is one of the reasons of the failures of TCP/IP in V2X
questions.answer_display: The increase in the number of wireless devices restricts the mobility

questions.question: CCN is derived from ICN architecture.
questions.detailed_solution:
CCN (Content Centric Networking) is derived from
Information Centric Networking (ICN)

questions.question: What are the disadvantages of V2X communication?
questions.detailed_solution:
Disadvantages of V2X communication includes tracking of
movement, violation of privacy, loss of data control, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is not done in traditional electrical grids?
questions.detailed_solution:
In Traditional electrical grids, the energy generation is done in centralized
power plants.

questions.question: Smart Grid is also named as-
questions.detailed_solution: Electronet is another name for smart grids

questions.question: Which of the following is not a property of smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart Grids uses information technology to deliver electricity efficiently,
reliably and securely

questions.question: Which of the following is the benefit of using Smart Grids for stakeholders?
questions.detailed_solution:
The smart grids reduce inefficiencies in energy delivery. This is one of the
benefits for stakeholders. The other benefits are for the customers

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ___________ samples voltage and current with a fixed sample rate at the installed location.
questions.detailed_solution:
PMUs or Phasor Measurement Unit samples voltage and current with a fixed
sample rate at the installed location

questions.question: Which of the following is a cloud application of smart grid?
questions.detailed_solution:
Energy management, information management and security are all the cloud
applications in smart grid

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about MDMSs? Statement I: Decide the price per unit energy to be paid by the customers Statement II: Handled by the energy service providers Statement III: Aggregate the energy consumption or energy request of certain geographical area
questions.detailed_solution:
The Meter Data Management Systems (MDMS) decide the price per unit
energy to be paid by the customers AND are handled by the energy service providers.

questions.question: Which of the following are included in the Smart Grid security issues of integrity? Statement I: System Damage Statement II: Data injection attacks Statement III: Time synchronization attacks
questions.detailed_solution:
System Damage, data injection attacks and time synchronization attacks are
the types of integrity issues in Smart Grids

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ is a primary challenge in IIoT.

options.text: Integrating existing infrastructure into new IIoT infrastructure

text: Integrating existing infrastructure into new IIoT infrastructure

questions.detailed_solution:
Integrating existing infrastructure into new IIoT infrastructure is one of the
primary challenges of IIoT.
questions.answer_display: Integrating existing infrastructure into new IIoT infrastructure

questions.question: Fill in the blank. Rt Tech particularizes in software which____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Rt Tech improves industrial facilities efficiency and productivity. It automates
the process of mapping and managing energy consumption

questions.question: Lack of vision and leadership is not a hindrance in the path of success of IIoT.
questions.detailed_solution:
Lack of vision and leadership is one of the hindrances in the path of success
of IIoT.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a vulnerability of Smart Grid?
questions.detailed_solution:
Integrity, physical threats and dynamic system attacks are all the vulnerabilities
of smart grids

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Big Data?
questions.detailed_solution: Big Data is characterized by 7 Vs, Veracity, Variability and Velocity included

questions.question: Variety refers to the category to which the data belongs.
questions.detailed_solution:
Variety refers to the category to which the data belongs. Examples: Pure text,
images, audio, etc.

questions.question: What is the Flow of data?
questions.detailed_solution:
The flow of the data is Generation, Acquisition, Storage and Analysis (Please
refer

questions.question: Quantitative analysis does not involve descriptive statistics such as mean.
questions.detailed_solution:
Quantitative analysis involves descriptive statistics such as mean, median and
standard deviation.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. To perform an ANOVA, we must have a _______ response variable and at least one _____ factor.
questions.detailed_solution:
To perform an ANOVA, we must have a continuous response variable and at
least one categorical factor.

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that denote the example of dispersion measure. Statement I: Range, Variable and Standard deviation Statement II: Range and average absolute deviation Statement III: Variance, Standard deviation and average absolute deviation
questions.detailed_solution:
The example of dispersion measure includes Range, Variable and Standard
deviation and average absolute deviation

questions.question: The different components of AgriSens includes -
questions.detailed_solution:
The different components of AgriSens includes sensor and actuator unit,
wireless communication unit, and power management unit.

questions.question: The two most relevant sensors directly used in agricultures are __________.

options.text: Soil moisture and water level sensor
ECG sensor and water level sensor

text: Soil moisture and water level sensor

questions.detailed_solution:
Soil moisture and water level sensors are the necessary sensors generally used
in agriculture.
questions.answer_display: Soil moisture and water level sensor

questions.question: The sensor node of AgriSens sense the average soil moisture in ______________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The sensor node of AgriSens sensed the average soil moisture in vegetative
phase, reproductive phase and maturity phase

questions.question: Select the correct order of the component layers present in the IoT healthcare.

options.text: Sensing layer, cloud platform layer, aggregated layer, processing layer
Sensing layer, aggregated layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer
Aggregated layer, sensing layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer
Sensing layer, processing layer, aggregated layer, cloud platform layer

text: Sensing layer, aggregated layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer

questions.detailed_solution:
The sensing layer senses data and transmit it o the aggregation layer where the
data are aggregated. The aggregated layer further transfers the data to the processing layer the data
are processed and final sent to the cloud platform.
questions.answer_display: Sensing layer, aggregated layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer

questions.question: Which of the following communication protocol is used in AmbuSens?
questions.detailed_solution:
In AmbuSens, the communication protocol used is Bluetooth i.e., IEEE
802.15.1

questions.question: Wireless IoT driven solutions for remote healthcare facility provisioning brings healthcare to patients than bringing patients to healthcare.
questions.detailed_solution:
In IoT healthcare, wireless IoT driven solutions brings healthcare to patients
than bringing patients to healthcare.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. The physical activity tracking is a necessary component for _____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The physical activity tracking is a necessary component for activity
monitoring

questions.question: Which of the following handheld devices are used for activity monitoring?
questions.detailed_solution:
High end smartphones are likely to have accelerometer, compass, and
gyroscope.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary disadvantage of using camera-based activity tracking?
questions.detailed_solution: Camera based activity tracking is very much process intensive.

questions.question: By performing continuous monitoring of a person's activity, it is not possible to observe his/her behavior or to identify any repetitive pattern in his/her day-to-day activity.
questions.detailed_solution:
Continuous monitoring of activity results in daily observations of human
behavior and repetitive patterns in their activity.

questions.question: Which of the following are the inbuilt sensors that are present in high end smartphones?
questions.detailed_solution:
High end smartphones are likely to have accelerometer, compass, and
gyroscope.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. Processing the handheld activity device data with artificial intelligence can be used for ________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Processing the handheld activity device data with artificial intelligence can be
used for detecting sudden fall of a person.

source_name: NPTEL - Introduction to Internet of Things

exam_pattern.practice_scoring_model: 1 mark per question inside this practice app

certification_weightage_hint.course_note: NPTEL course pages show that best-assignment aggregation varies by course, while certification commonly uses 25% assignment and 75% proctored exam weightage.
